description: Confounder: The model assumes the tensor is shaped (num_experts, hidden_size, intermediate_d

_meta.explanation: The rule 'SwiGLUExperts.reset_parameters initializes gate_proj and up_proj weights with std=1/sqrt(hidden_size), not using kaiming_uniform_’s default fan_in computation, because kaiming_uniform_ misinterprets the first dimension of 3-D tensors as fan_out.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes the tensor is shaped (num_experts, hidden_size, intermediate_dim) as in the rule, so it applies gate_std=1/sqrt(hidden_size). But the tensor is actually shaped (num_experts, intermediate_dim, hidden_size), so when later used as up_proj/gate_proj, the effective fan_in dimension is now 128, not 64 — yet the std was still set using hidden_size=64.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'SwiGLUExperts.reset_parameters initializes gate_proj and up_proj weights with std=1/sqrt(hidden_size), not using kaiming_uniform_’s default fan_in computation, because kaiming_uniform_ misinterprets the first dimension of 3-D tensors as fan_out.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
